task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: A flower image is loaded in GIMP
task_completion.visual_evidence: A photo of a flower is visible in the main GIMP image canvas.
The Layers dockable dialog is present in the GIMP interface (typically on the right side).

task_completion.description: A layer is selected in the Layers dialog
task_completion.visual_evidence: A specific layer is highlighted in the Layers dialog, indicating it is the active layer.

task_completion.description: The active layer opacity is adjusted to 65%
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Opacity slider or numeric input box in the Layers dialog shows a value of 65 (values between 62 and 68 are acceptable).

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP GUI to perform the task
integrity.visual_evidence: The GIMP interface is visible and was interacted with, rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use scripts to bypass GUI interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible showing ImageMagick, Python, or other scripts being used to alter the image opacity directly.

integrity.description: The actual flower image is being edited
integrity.visual_evidence: The image in the GIMP canvas is a genuine photo (a flower), not a blank canvas or a text placeholder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target opacity is 65%, with an acceptable tolerance range of 62% to 68%. The task must be performed in GIMP on a flower image. Verify the opacity by checking the numeric value on the Opacity slider in the GIMP Layers dialog.
